n_trials.note: DSR verdict is identical (dsr=0.0, FAIL) whether n_trials=2 (this run only) or n_trials=5 (cumulative family) -- the observed OOS Sharpe is far below the threshold the deflated-Sharpe formula requires at either trial count, so the n_trials choice is not the deciding factor here.

canary.note: signal at/before the corrupted bar must be unchanged by a same-bar spike

shift_collapse.note: PASS: Sharpe stable under an extra bar of lag (no look-ahead signature)

shift_collapse.note: PASS: Sharpe stable under an extra bar of lag (no look-ahead signature)

verdict.reason: Both symbols FAIL the flat-cost gate's deflated-Sharpe test (dsr=0.00 < 0.95 threshold at n_trials=2 and n_trials=5) despite positive OOS Sharpe and 2x-fee/delayed-fill stress survival. The honest maker-fill simulation confirms the drawdown-control property survives fill friction (fill_rate=1.00 on the base and offset variants -- regime-flip transition bars trade through easily since they occur on momentum days), and 2x fees / delayed fills only mildly compress Sharpe. But under the harness's own strict DSR bar this family does not clear PASS. This is the SAME 'FAIL (retail) but real drawdown-control' result the June run found, now confirmed under an honest fill model rather than flat costs.
